results.title: Hilton's CIO: Building the AI Trip Planner and Grappling With 'Tokenomics'
highlights:
# Hilton's CIO: Building the AI Trip Planner and Grappling With 'Tokenomics'
[...]
Hilton's CIO: Building the AI Trip Planner and Grappling With 'Tokenomics'
[...]
A live AI tool with early conversion gains is one Hilton story. The cost of running AI across the wider company is another.
[...]
- How is Hilton managing the costs associated with large language model usage and 'tokenomics'?
- How did Hilton modernize its technology infrastructure to support AI tools?
- What architectural requirements are necessary for effective AI integration in hospitality, according to Hilton's CIO?
[...]
Hilton’s AI trip planning tool is delivering “slightly higher” conversion rates three months after launch, and the hotel group is preparing to roll the tool into the Hilton Honors loyalty app this month.
[...]
“The first iteration, it would allow one prompt and then immediately ask for dates,” said Michael Leidinger, Hilton’s chief information officer, at the Skift Data + AI Summit on Wednesday. “I told the team, that’s not how people think.”
[...]
The AI Planner is built on Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet and trained on Hilton’s property content. The rebuilt version lets travelers iterate across several turns before the tool narrows toward specific properties and dates.
[...]
“For years, we’ve been trying to figure out how do we crack that dreaming piece,” he said. “This is a great example of where technology and the advancements in AI have really allowed us to make some architectural leaps.”
[...]
Leidinger said Hilton spent roughly eight years rebuilding the stack — breaking up a legacy monolithic system, moving to a modern API and microservices framework, and shifting close to 90% of its compute to the cloud.
[...]
Leidinger pointed to two architectural requirements for AI to work: a data layer he called “the lubricant of AI,” and an event-driven architecture that lets systems pick up and act on signals in real time.
[...]
“You absolutely need that data to drive it,” he said. “But the eventing architecture is also critical.”
[...]
Cost is the next constraint. Large language model providers are pushing harder on monetization, and Leidinger said the spend is showing up across the company.
[...]
“Everyone right now is talking tokenomics,” he said. “These LLM companies have spent hundreds of billions of dollars, and they are now leaning into monetization. So this does come with a cost.”
[...]
Hilton is tracking token consumption across the company and pushing back on internal use that doesn’t justify the spend.
[...]
“A lot of people are turning to AI just to use it, but it’s not really necessarily the right thing to use it for,” Leidinger said. “As you deploy, this is part of the change management and education program you have to do throughout the entire organization.”
[...]
### Hilton and Air Canada at Skift Data + AI Summit 2026
[...]
If you are funding AI features before the data and event architecture beneath them, this session argued you are building on sand.

Hilton Hotels & Resorts, the flagship brand of Hilton Worldwide, operates over 600 upscale hotels and resorts globally, employing 5,000–10,000 team members directly within the brand division. # Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity) (Remote)

Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity)
[...]
### Location: United States
[...]
Department: Data Science
[...]
Trust is the foundation of a vibrant Airbnb community. The Content Integrity Data Science team helps build and protect that trust by ensuring that the content on Airbnb, including listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated experiences, is accurate, authentic and aligned with our policies and community standards.
[...]
We partner closely with product, engineering, policy and operations teams to develop advanced AI-driven content understanding systems, scale effective human-in-the-loop workflows and proactively defend against emerging content risks, so guests and hosts can interact with confidence.
[...]
This role is unique in that it directly improves the safety, trust, and quality of real-world user experiences by advancing Airbnb’s ability to understand, interpret and act on content at scale. You will help shape how the platform reasons about listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated content by building the next generation of Trust Content Understanding Models.
[...]
- The ideal candidate is a motivated and talented “full-stack” Data Scientist with strong applied ML intuition and a bias toward impact, who can own and drive forward challenging, high-visibility initiatives such as:
[...]
- Advance Airbnb’s content integrity capabilities by building Natural Language Processing (NLP) and LLM-based models that understand intent, policy compliance, quality and risk across listings, profiles, and user communications
[...]
- Develop high-performing models for detecting problematic or misleading content, including text classification, semantic similarity, information extraction and generative model-based reasoning for policy interpretation and enforcement
[...]
- Design and optimize human-in-the-loop Machine Learning (ML) systems for content review, labeling, escalation and continuous model improvement
[...]
- Build systems to detect emerging content risks and abuse patterns across regions, cohorts and surfaces using statistical, ML and representation-learning approaches
[...]
- Design intelligent sampling and evaluation strategies to measure rare events, policy recall, false positives/negatives and model blind spots in large-
[...]
- Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning: Build and deploy production AI/ML systems for content integrity and trust content understanding, including feature engineering, model development and evaluation, thresholding, error analysis and end-to-end model lifecycle management. This includes working with NLP and LLM-based models in real production settings.
[...]
- 5+ years of industry experience in a quantitative analysis role with a Master’s degree in a quantitative field (computer science, statistics etc.), or 2+ years of experience with a Ph.D.
[...]
- State-of-the-art knowledge of AI/ML models
- Hands-on experience building, evaluating, and deploying NLP and LLM-based solutions, including text classification, information extraction, semantic understanding or generative applications.
[...]
- Working knowledge of
[...]
